_doc: Release-level metadata for the unified changelog ‚Äî the DATE and the 'what shipped' SUMMARY for each version. Per-package change details (Added/Changed/Fixed/Removed) live in each package's CHANGELOG.md and are aggregated by scripts/generate-changelog.mjs; this file supplies the release date + summary that the per-package files don't carry. Hand-maintained: add one entry per release, keyed by the version string. `date` is a display string (e.g. "June 4, 2026"); `summary` supports inline `code`, **bold**, and [links](url); `featured` (optional) is an array of package names floated to the top of that release's package list, in the listed order (the rest follow most-changed-first, then alphabetical).

4.3.0.summary: Self-update tooling for the framework. **`@warlock.js/core`** adds the `warlock update` command ‚Äî it bumps every `@warlock.js/*` package in your project's `package.json` to the latest version (preserving each range operator) and runs your package manager's install ‚Äî and `warlock dev` now prints a non-blocking notice when a newer release is published, toggleable via `devServer.checkForUpdates`. **`@warlock.js/ai`** ships the capstone of the primitive ladder: `ai.orchestrator()` for durable, drift-detecting, resumable multi-turn sessions (with checkpoint + snapshot stores via `ai.checkpoint.*` / `ai.snapshot.*`, history compaction, and a 3-tier event surface), plus `ai.planner()` (an LLM generates an ordered plan over your capabilities, then runs it), `ai.spawnSubAgent()`, and `ai.memory()` (working / semantic / episodic / procedural recall). A cost-truth pass lands across all five provider adapters (`Usage.reasoningTokens`, per-channel pricing, reasoning / prompt-cache controls), alongside DX helpers ‚Äî `ai.batch`, `ai.router`, `ai.fanOut`, `ai.fallbackModel`, `ai.mockRouter`, `agent.eval` + Vitest matchers, and `systemPrompt.fromFile`. ‚öÝ **Breaking:** supervisor + workflow snapshot persistence moved from a `CacheDriver` to the dedicated `SnapshotStore` contract (`snapshotStore: ai.snapshot.<tier>(‚Ä¶)`; the fallback is now `ai.config({ defaultSnapshotStore })`). A new package, **`@warlock.js/ai-panoptic`**, adds observability ‚Äî a collector that projects run reports into traces and exports them to OpenTelemetry, Langfuse, the console, a file, or a queryable in-memory store.

4.2.11.summary: Soft deletes go end-to-end in **`@warlock.js/cascade`** ‚Äî `Migration.create` auto-wires the `deletedAt` column for soft-delete models and soft `destroy()` keeps the in-memory model in sync. **`@warlock.js/core`** adds the `lowerStage3Decorators()` test plugin and `warlock add notifications`; **`@warlock.js/notifications`** gains model-driven `columnMap`, presence-based read-state, and multi-tenant support. The whole family ‚Äî and the `create-warlock` project template ‚Äî moves to `@mongez/reinforcements` 3.3.0.

4.2.10.summary: Patch release: **`@warlock.js/auth`** moves `@mongez/copper` / `@mongez/events` / `@mongez/reinforcements` from peer to regular dependencies, clearing the `unmet peer dependency` warnings on install ‚Äî they're framework-internal utilities your app never provides. **`@warlock.js/logger`**'s console timestamp switches to a cleaner 256-color `slate` gray. **`create-warlock`** ships the latest `@mongez/*` versions in its project template. The rest of the family is re-published at 4.2.10 to keep the lockstep version line.

4.2.9.summary: Patch release: **`@warlock.js/logger`**'s `ConsoleLog` is retuned for scannability ‚Äî a time-only timestamp (`HH:mm:ss.SSS`) dimmed to gray, fixed-width level tags so the timestamp / module / action columns align, and `fatal` restored to a white-on-bright-red **background badge** so it can't be missed next to `error`. The rest of the family is re-published at 4.2.9 to keep the lockstep version line; no other functional changes.

4.2.8.summary: Patch release: **`@warlock.js/logger`**'s `ConsoleLog` now prints the level name beside each icon (`‚öô debug`, `‚Ñπ info`, `‚öÝ warn`, `‚úó error`, `‚úì success`, `‚òÝ fatal`) for at-a-glance readability; `fatal` drops the bright-red background badge for a bold red-bright `‚òÝ fatal` label. The rest of the family is re-published at 4.2.8 to keep the lockstep version line; no other functional changes.

4.2.7.summary: Patch release: **`create-warlock`** now ships its `templates/` folder, so scaffolding a new project works from the installed package ‚Äî it was missing from the build, which made the wizard fail with "Something went wrong" at the template-copy step. The rest of the family is re-published at 4.2.7 to keep the lockstep version line; no other functional changes.

4.2.6.summary: Patch release: **`create-warlock`** now ships its `bin` folder again, so the `create-warlock` CLI works from the installed package ‚Äî it was omitted from the 4.2.5 build. The rest of the family is re-published at 4.2.6 to keep the lockstep version line; no other functional changes.

4.2.5.summary: Patch release: `warlock add notifications` now scaffolds the in-app read/dismiss HTTP surface ‚Äî `routes.ts` + a notifications controller (list / unread-count / mark-read / mark-all-read / clear / delete), gated by `authMiddleware` and recipient-scoped via `inApp`. The family is re-published in lockstep; no other functional changes.

4.2.4.summary: Patch release: corrects the worker-loader path in **`@warlock.js/core`**'s build entry points ‚Äî a wrong path in 4.2.3 left the worker entry broken. The family is re-published in lockstep; no other functional changes.

4.2.3.summary: Patch release: adds the worker scripts as **`@warlock.js/core`** build entry points so they ship in the published package. A wrong entry-point path in this build blocked the npm publish for some packages ‚Äî corrected in 4.2.4.

4.2.2.summary: Patch release: adds `cli/start` to **`@warlock.js/core`**'s build entry points so the `warlock` CLI entry ships in the published package. The family is re-published in lockstep; no other functional changes.

4.2.1.summary: Patch release: **`@warlock.js/core`** and **`@warlock.js/cascade`** now ship their `bin` folder, so the `warlock` and `cascade` CLIs work from the published package ‚Äî they were omitted from the 4.2.0 build. The rest of the family is re-published at 4.2.1 to keep the lockstep version line; no other functional changes.

4.2.0.summary: A security and correctness overhaul of `@warlock.js/auth` ‚Äî brute-force login throttling, atomic refresh-token rotation with replay detection, CSPRNG signing secrets, a corrected default token lifetime, and overridable token storage for multi-tenant schemas. The `jwt` config block is superseded by `accessToken` / `refreshToken`. Also introduces **`@warlock.js/notifications`** ‚Äî multi-channel notifications (mail, in-app, custom channels) with preferences, rate limits, idempotency, and a herald-backed async queue. Plus **`@warlock.js/access`** ‚Äî authorization (RBAC + ABAC): permission checks, attribute-based policies, role management, and a pluggable resolver.

4.1.15.summary: Initial public baseline of the **Warlock.js** framework ‚Äî 17 packages published in lockstep at `4.1.15`. From this release on, every change is recorded in its package's `CHANGELOG.md` and aggregated here by version.
